sanskrit: अव्यक्तं व्यक्तिमापन्नं मन्यन्ते मामबुद्धयः। परं भावमजानन्तो ममाव्ययमनुत्तमम्॥
transliteration: avyaktaṃ vyaktim āpannaṃ manyante mām abuddhayaḥ | paraṃ bhāvam ajānanto mamāvyayam anuttamam ||
literal_meaning: Unintelligent ones, not knowing My supreme, imperishable, unsurpassed essence, think that I, the Unmanifest, have assumed a manifest form.
contextual_meaning: People with limited cognitive-emotional bandwidth mistake the therapist’s visible persona (body, voice, office) for the totality of the therapist’s being, unaware of the deeper, changeless, trans-personal presence that holds the therapeutic space.
psychological_state: Arjuna is beginning to sense that the Divine cannot be shrunk to fit human categories; a subtle dissonance arises between his habitual mental maps and the living Reality in front of him.
trigger_scenario: A client insists the therapist is ‘just a paid listener’ and dismisses any talk of unconditional presence, thereby missing the felt sense of being held by something larger than the hourly fee.
anchor_text: Look past the therapist’s form; feel the silent, changeless presence holding you.
